title: Opinions on City Tourism
transcript: Part 1. You're going to hear five people. You'll hear each person only once. First, read items 41 to 45. After you've listened to each person, decide if the statement for each of the items 41 to 45 is true or not true. Mark your answer on the answer sheet. Now, you will have half a minute to read the five statements. Part 2. You're going to hear five people talking about the impact of tourism on their city. Number 41. Honestly, it's getting a bit too much. I've lived in this neighborhood my whole life, and now I can't even walk down the street without bumping into groups of people taking photos. The noise level is high day and night, and the local shops have become very expensive. I really miss the quiet days before we became so popular. Number 42. For me, tourism is a blessing. I own a small souvenir shop near the cathedral, and 90% of my customers are visitors. If they stopped coming, I would have to close my business immediately. They bring money into the city and create jobs for young people. We should be grateful for them. Number 43. It is a shame to see how our city is changing. We are losing our traditional culture because everything is designed for tourists now. Also, the large tour buses cause a lot of pollution, and the crowds are actually damaging the historic buildings. We need stricter rules to protect our heritage. Number 44. The biggest problem is finding a place to live. Landlords prefer to rent their apartments to tourists for short holidays because they can charge more money. As a student, I cannot afford the high rents anymore. I might have to move to a different town just to afford a room. Number 45. I actually enjoy the atmosphere. It is exciting to hear so many different languages on the bus or in the park. I have met wonderful people from all over the world right here in my hometown. It makes the city feel more international and alive, which I think is a great thing. End of Part 1

statements.statement: The speaker enjoys the lively atmosphere created by the tourists.
statements.audio_transcript: Honestly, it's getting a bit too much. I've lived in this neighborhood my whole life, and now I can't even walk down the street without bumping into groups of people taking photos. The noise level is high day and night, and the local shops have become very expensive. I really miss the quiet days before we became so popular.

explanation.de: FALSCH. Der Sprecher sagt, es werde «ein bisschen zu viel» und er vermisse «die ruhigen Tage, bevor wir so beliebt wurden». Er beschwert sich über Lärm und teure Geschäfte – er genießt die Atmosphäre eindeutig nicht.
explanation.en: INCORRECT. The speaker says 'it's getting a bit too much' and 'I really miss the quiet days before we became so popular'. They complain about noise and expensive shops — they clearly do not enjoy the atmosphere.
explanation.ar: خطأ. يقول المتحدث 'الأمر أصبح مبالغاً فيه قليلاً' و'أفتقد الأيام الهادئة قبل أن نصبح مشهورين'. يشتكي من الضوضاء والغلاء – واضح أنه لا يستمتع بالأجواء.
explanation.fr: FAUX. Le locuteur dit que 'ça devient un peu trop' et qu'il 'regrette les jours calmes avant qu'on devienne si populaires'. Il se plaint du bruit et des magasins chers – il n'apprécie clairement pas l'ambiance.
explanation.es: INCORRECTO. El hablante dice que 'se está volviendo demasiado' y 'extraño los días tranquilos antes de que nos volviéramos tan populares'. Se queja del ruido y las tiendas caras – claramente no disfruta el ambiente.
explanation.ru: НЕПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит, что 'это становится слишком' и 'я скучаю по спокойным дням, когда мы не были так популярны'. Он жалуется на шум и дорогие магазины – он явно не наслаждается атмосферой.

statements.statement: The speaker depends on visitors for their livelihood.
statements.audio_transcript: For me, tourism is a blessing. I own a small souvenir shop near the cathedral, and 90% of my customers are visitors. If they stopped coming, I would have to close my business immediately. They bring money into the city and create jobs for young people. We should be grateful for them.

explanation.de: RICHTIG. Der Sprecher sagt, er besitze ein Souvenirladen und «90% meiner Kunden sind Besucher». Wenn sie nicht mehr kämen, müsste er «sofort schließen». Er ist also völlig von Touristen abhängig.
explanation.en: CORRECT. The speaker says '90% of my customers are visitors' and 'if they stopped coming, I would have to close my business immediately'. They are entirely dependent on tourists for their income.
explanation.ar: صحيح. يقول المتحدث إن '90% من زبائني زوار' و'إذا توقفوا عن القدوم، سأضطر لإغلاق عملي فوراً'. إنه يعتمد كلياً على السياح في دخله.
explanation.fr: CORRECT. Le locuteur dit que '90% de mes clients sont des visiteurs' et 'si ils arrêtaient de venir, je devrais fermer mon commerce immédiatement'. Il dépend entièrement des touristes pour vivre.
explanation.es: CORRECTO. El hablante dice que 'el 90% de mis clientes son visitantes' y 'si dejaran de venir, tendría que cerrar mi negocio inmediatamente'. Depende totalmente de los turistas para su sustento.
explanation.ru: ПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит, что '90% моих клиентов — посетители' и 'если они перестанут приходить, мне придётся сразу закрыться'. Он полностью зависит от туристов.

statements.statement: The speaker believes tourism is harming local culture and buildings.
statements.audio_transcript: It is a shame to see how our city is changing. We are losing our traditional culture because everything is designed for tourists now. Also, the large tour buses cause a lot of pollution, and the crowds are actually damaging the historic buildings. We need stricter rules to protect our heritage.

explanation.de: RICHTIG. Der Sprecher sagt, «wir verlieren unsere traditionelle Kultur» und die Menschenmassen «beschädigen die historischen Gebäude». Er fordert «strengere Regeln zum Schutz unseres Erbes».
explanation.en: CORRECT. The speaker says 'we are losing our traditional culture because everything is designed for tourists' and 'the crowds are actually damaging the historic buildings'. They clearly believe tourism is harming both culture and buildings.
explanation.ar: صحيح. يقول المتحدث 'نحن نفقد ثقافتنا التقليدية' و'الحشود تتسبب فعلاً في إتلاف المباني التاريخية'. هو يعتقد بوضوح أن السياحة تضر الثقافة والمباني.
explanation.fr: CORRECT. Le locuteur dit 'nous perdons notre culture traditionnelle' et 'les foules endommagent les bâtiments historiques'. Il croit clairement que le tourisme nuit à la culture et aux bâtiments.
explanation.es: CORRECTO. El hablante dice 'estamos perdiendo nuestra cultura tradicional' y 'las multitudes están dañando los edificios históricos'. Claramente cree que el turismo perjudica la cultura y los edificios.
explanation.ru: ПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит, что 'мы теряем нашу традиционную культуру' и 'толпы реально повреждают исторические здания'. Он явно считает, что туризм вредит культуре и зданиям.

statements.statement: Tourism has made it easier for students to find accommodation.
statements.audio_transcript: The biggest problem is finding a place to live. Landlords prefer to rent their apartments to tourists for short holidays because they can charge more money. As a student, I cannot afford the high rents anymore. I might have to move to a different town just to afford a room.

explanation.de: FALSCH. Der Sprecher sagt das Gegenteil: Vermieter bevorzugen Touristen, weil sie «mehr Geld verlangen können». Als Student kann er sich «die hohen Mieten nicht mehr leisten». Der Tourismus hat also das Wohnen schwieriger gemacht, nicht einfacher.
explanation.en: INCORRECT. The speaker says the opposite: landlords prefer tourists because 'they can charge more money'. As a student, they 'cannot afford the high rents anymore' and might have to move. Tourism has made accommodation harder, not easier.
explanation.ar: خطأ. يقول المتحدث العكس: الملاك يفضلون السياح لأنهم 'يمكنهم طلب مزيد من المال'. كطالب، 'لا يستطيع تحمل الإيجارات المرتفعة بعد الآن'. السياحة جعلت السكن أصعب.
explanation.fr: FAUX. Le locuteur dit le contraire : les propriétaires préfèrent les touristes car 'ils peuvent demander plus d'argent'. En tant qu'étudiant, il 'ne peut plus se permettre les loyers élevés'. Le tourisme a rendu le logement plus difficile, pas plus facile.
explanation.es: INCORRECTO. El hablante dice lo contrario: los propietarios prefieren a los turistas porque 'pueden cobrar más dinero'. Como estudiante, 'ya no puede pagar los alquileres altos'. El turismo ha hecho más difícil encontrar alojamiento, no más fácil.
explanation.ru: НЕПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит обратное: арендодатели предпочитают туристов, потому что 'могут брать больше денег'. Как студент, он 'больше не может позволить высокую аренду'. Туризм усложнил поиск жилья.

statements.statement: The speaker likes meeting people from different countries.
statements.audio_transcript: I actually enjoy the atmosphere. It is exciting to hear so many different languages on the bus or in the park. I have met wonderful people from all over the world right here in my hometown. It makes the city feel more international and alive, which I think is a great thing.

explanation.de: RICHTIG. Der Sprecher sagt, er habe «wunderbare Menschen aus der ganzen Welt» in seiner Heimatstadt getroffen. Er findet es «aufregend» und «großartig», dass die Stadt internationaler wird.
explanation.en: CORRECT. The speaker says 'I have met wonderful people from all over the world right here in my hometown' and finds it 'exciting to hear so many different languages'. They clearly enjoy meeting people from different countries.
explanation.ar: صحيح. يقول المتحدث 'قابلت أشخاصاً رائعين من جميع أنحاء العالم هنا في مدينتي'. يجد الأمر 'مثيراً' و'شيئاً رائعاً'. هو يستمتع بوضوح بلقاء أشخاص من بلدان مختلفة.
explanation.fr: CORRECT. Le locuteur dit avoir 'rencontré des gens merveilleux du monde entier ici dans ma ville'. Il trouve 'émouvant d'entendre tant de langues différentes'. Il apprécie clairement la rencontre avec des gens de différents pays.
explanation.es: CORRECTO. El hablante dice 'he conocido a personas maravillosas de todo el mundo aquí en mi ciudad'. Encuentra 'emocionante escuchar tantos idiomas diferentes'. Claramente disfruta conocer gente de diferentes países.
explanation.ru: ПРАВИЛЬНО. Говорящий говорит, что 'встретил замечательных людей со всего мира здесь, в своём городе'. Ему 'интересно слышать столько языков'. Он явно любит знакомиться с людьми из разных стран.
